mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive forward returns exceed 55% during rate hike periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp quarterly increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending capacity, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 60% of instances when the unemployment rate crosses the 0.5pp threshold over consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real personal income and stable consumer price inflation indicates robust household purchasing power without monetary erosion, which sustains corporate profit margins and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative forward returns in over 50% of occurrences or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: An expanding industrial production index reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which signals near-term earnings revisions upward and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict positive returns during recovery phases, with a negative Rank IC over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding economic activity and rising wage bills, which supports consumer demand and corporate revenue growth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth consistently coincides with negative equity returns, yielding a Rank IC below 0.01 across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A significant year-over-year contraction in housing starts signals a sharp pullback in residential investment and construction activity, which reduces upstream supply chain revenues and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Housing starts decline by more than 10% annually while S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in over 65% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising federal funds rates and increasing unemployment creates a stagflationary pressure that simultaneously raises financing costs and suppresses economic output, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition produces positive forward returns in the majority of observations or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth reflects broad-based economic expansion and rising corporate profit potential, which drives equity valuation multiples higher and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP acceleration consistently precedes equity drawdowns, with forward returns negative in over 55% of triggered quarters.
